_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: External fixation of Fracture - Both bones -forearms
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Immobilize a particular part of the body following a fracture or with certain orthopedic problems to allow bone healing.
Fixation and manipulation of multiple bone segments which would otherwise be very difficult to manage.
Used in orthopedic trauma, pediatric orthopedics, and plastic surgery for an array of different pathology.
Comminuted periarticular fractures such as pilon, distal femur, tibial plateau, elbow, and distal radius fractures
Fractures with large amounts of soft tissue swelling
Fractures which hemodynamically unstable or cannot undergo an open procedure
Open fractures with soft tissue loss
Immobilization of joint after soft tissue flap
Arthrodesis, Nonunion, Malunion, Infection, traction to aid in intraoperative fracture reduction.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with indication for surgery
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, and indication for procedure?
Clinical photograph of affected part submitted?
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part confirm the diagnosis?
Was clinical presentation and X-ray report of affected part indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation and X-ray report of affected part not indicative of surgery.
X-ray not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right) of affected part, or diagnosis not confirmed.
Clinical notes missing detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned treatment, or indication for procedure.
Invoice or barcode of implant not submitted at claim stage.
Post-operative clinical photographs not submitted at claim stage.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) lacking daily vitals and treatment details.

clinical_key_pointers: Physicians have been using External fixation to treat fractures for more than 2000 years, it helps to immobilize a particular part of the body following a fracture or with certain orthopedic problems to allow bone healing.
The fixation and manipulation of multiple bone segments which would otherwise be very difficult to manage.
Usually the material involved in this process are Pins, wires, self-drilling/tapping, and braces, clamps, Rods, Ring components, used to immobilize the part, Uni, biplanar, biomechanics,

Types of fixation evolved over period which includes; First Generation (Classic rigid A frame) (Unilateral Fixator), second generation, third generation (Circular fine wire fixator, Circular external fixation), fourth generation (Mobile unilateral external fixators), Fifth generation (Multiplanar Fixation with Multiaxial Correction) and hybrid fixation.
